validators_run.name: android_framework_runs_and_produces_valid_report
validators_run.stdout_tail:
Results: 70/70 passed, 9 skipped, 4 warnings
PASS — all extracted blocks compile cleanly

notes:
Framework created and all 70 extracted blocks now compile cleanly against the real Amity Android SDK JARs.

Stats: 70 extracted, 9 skipped (build-script fragments, Android UI/RecyclerView blocks, class-body-only fragments), 70 passed, 0 failed.

Doc bugs found and fixed:
- authentication.mdx: .sessionHandler() builder method doesn't exist → SessionHandler is a login() parameter; AmitySessionState.* → SessionState.* sealed class; removed pseudocode authRepository/myApiService stubs; auth-block-7 accessToken → authToken
- android-quick-start.mdx: Gradle DSL block skipped with marker (not runtime Kotlin)
- edit-post.mdx block-1: postData.edit() on AmityPost.Data.TEXT returns AmityTextPostUpdate.Builder (no .links() method) → fixed to postRepository.editPost() which returns AmityPostUpdate.Builder (has .links()); blocks 3&4: AmityAttachmentProductTags() constructor is internal → fixed to Builder pattern; AmityLink() missing required params (index, length, renderPreview, domain, imageUrl) → provided all; .attachments(image) referenced undefined 'image' → fixed to .attachments(*attachments.toTypedArray())
- delete-post.mdx: postRepository.deletePost() doesn't exist → hardDeletePost()
- query-posts.mdx: .getPagingData() doesn't exist on query builder → .query(); Calendar.getInstance().time (java.util.Date) passed to untilAt(DateTime) → org.joda.time.DateTime().minusDays(7)
- query-members.mdx block-2: channel?.getChannelId() on nullable receiver → use channelId param directly; block-4: Observable<PagingData<AmityChannelMember>> → Flowable (SDK returns Flowable)
- send-a-message.mdx block-2: when(error.code){AmityError.BAN_WORD_FOUND→} — AmityError enum vs Int mismatch → AmityError.BAN_WORD_FOUND.code; block-5: AmityChatClient.setup() doesn't exist → AmityCoreClient.setup()
- get-user-information.mdx: userRepository.getUsers() returns Builder, not Flowable → .getUsers().build().query().doOnNext{}
- update-user-information.mdx: amityImage undefined placeholder → explicit uploadedAvatar: AmityImage parameter
- viewing-content.mdx: when(AmityPost.Data) missing else branch → added else -> {}
- query-and-filter-messages.mdx: PagingDataAdapter block skipped

Framework key design:
- Compiler: Kotlin 1.9.22 + -Xskip-metadata-version-check (SDK compiled with Kotlin 2.2.0 metadata)
- Classpath: amity-sdk + amity-rxbridge + amity-log + rxjava3 + android.jar + kotlin-stdlib + annotations + rxandroid + paging-common + gson + joda-time + reactive-streams
- Preamble: 45+ imports, 17 stub helper functions, 16 docSnippet() default params
- Added import: com.amity.socialcloud.sdk.helper.core.asAmityFile/asAmityImage/asAmityVideo extension functions

Follow-up: wire Android into .docs-ops/ci/check-drift.py (see pending task).
